working_definition: A group 3 innate lymphoid cell present in prenatal human skin from early gestation, characterized by expression of the transcription factor RORgammat (RORC). In this atlas, ILC3 cells are predicted to co-locate and interact with pre-dermal condensate cells through CXCL12-CXCR4 and CXCL12-DPP4 ligand-receptor signals, suggesting a role in hair follicle morphogenesis. ILC3 are RORgammat-dependent innate lymphoid cells capable of producing IL-17 and/or IL-22.
searches_performed: OLS4 searchClasses: 'group 3 innate lymphoid cell' in CL ontology
snippet_search: 'ILC3 innate lymphoid cell type 3 prenatal skin' scoped to atlas DOI:10.1038/s41586-024-08002-x
snippet_search: 'ILC3 group 3 innate lymphoid cell fetal prenatal skin RORC RORgammat development hair follicle' broad search

best_match.cl_definition: An innate lymphoid cell that constituitively expresses RORgt and is capable of expressing IL17A and/or IL-22.

justification: The ILC3 population identified in the prenatal skin atlas is characterized as an RORgammat-dependent innate lymphoid cell that co-locates with pre-dermal condensate cells. The CL:0001071 definition describes an innate lymphoid cell that constitutively expresses RORgammat and is capable of expressing IL-17A and/or IL-22, which matches the atlas ILC3 identity. While the atlas ILC3 population likely includes both LTi-like and non-LTi subsets (as shown by Liu et al., 2021 in fetal tissues), the general CL:0001071 term appropriately captures the cell type identity at the resolution provided in this atlas.
recommendation: Map to CL:0001071 (group 3 innate lymphoid cell). No new CL term is needed.
